title: Visually impaired runner takes on London Marathon
highlightedText: A visually impaired runner from North Yorkshire is preparing to take on the London Marathon in April to raise money for charity.
desc: Calum McCready, 38, from Knaresborough, was diagnosed with the degenerative eye condition retinitis pigmentosa when he was 17. He is preparing to participate in the London Marathon in April to raise funds for charity. The story highlights his journey and determination as a visually impaired athlete aiming to inspire others and support charitable causes.

title: How Blind Ambition Empowers the Blind and Low Vision Community
highlightedText: We’re building a space where visually impaired people can find career opportunities, training, and community support all in one place.
desc: Blind Ambition has launched a web-based talent pool for people with vision loss, providing access to job listings, training, and community support. The initiative aims to connect visually impaired job seekers with employers and foster a more inclusive workforce, while also raising awareness about the challenges and achievements of the blind and low-vision community.

title: Visually Impaired Receive Life Changing Tech
highlightedText: On Tuesday, more than 10 people received the gift of better vision thanks to receiving high-tech vision aids.
desc: Over 10 visually impaired individuals in Decatur, Alabama, received advanced assistive devices such as the OrCam My Eye, which uses a camera and artificial intelligence to recognize faces, read text, and identify objects. The devices, distributed through Sight Savers America, are provided at no cost to recipients and aim to increase independence and quality of life for people with severe visual impairment.

title: AI-boosted Cameras Help Blind People to Navigate
highlightedText: A system that helps visually impaired people to navigate their surroundings using cameras, earphones and artificial intelligence (AI) could offer advantages over white canes and other conventional technologies.
desc: Researchers have developed a prototype wearable system that uses cameras and AI to help visually impaired users navigate obstacles. Tested by 20 participants, the device improved walking distance and navigation time by 25% compared to traditional canes. The system provides real-time audio and vibration feedback, offering a new level of independence for users.

title: Challenges Faced by Visually Impaired Pupils in Mainstream Schools in Malawi
highlightedText: Students with visual impairments may face diverse obstacles with curricular features such as topic, language or medium of instruction, instructional tempo and time constraints, learning materials, and assessment methods.
desc: A study in Malawi highlights the difficulties visually impaired students encounter in mainstream secondary schools, including rigid curricula, inadequate support materials, and inaccessible environments. The research calls for inclusive education training for teachers and better resources to ensure equal learning opportunities for all students.

title: The Ultimate Guide to the Best Assistive Technology for People Who Are Blind or Have Low Vision in 2025
highlightedText: Envision Glasses are a groundbreaking assistive tool for people who are blind or have low vision. Combining AI-powered features with a sleek, wearable design, they deliver unparalleled versatility.
desc: The article reviews the latest hardware and software solutions for the blind and visually impaired, including Envision Glasses, navigation apps, and text-to-speech programs. These technologies are enhancing independence, accessibility, and inclusion for users worldwide.
